data.title: Volvo Has Limited Exposure to Improving U.S. — Market Talk
data.description: Volvo has limited exposure to an improving U.S. truck market versus peers, and shares look fully valued, RBC Capital Markets analyst Nick Housden writes. Volvo's limited U.S. dependence was highly desirable last year due to U.S. tariffs and given wider North American freight headwinds. "At this point in the cycle, we prefer names with more North American exposure as the market there inflects." Meanwhile, after recent share outperformance, Volvo trades well above 10-year average multiples. "We do not see Volvo's earnings right now as especially depressed. As such, a further re-rating seems unlikely." RBC downgrades Volvo to sector perform from outperform and lifts its price target to 360 Swedish kronor from 350 kronor. Shares closed at 348.20 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Sumitomo Forestry to Buy Tri Pointe Homes for $4.1 Billion
data.description:
(07:16 GMT) *Sumitomo Forestry: To Buy Tri Pointe Homes for $4.28B
(07:18 GMT) *Sumitomo Forestry: Tri Pointe Homes Stockholders to Receive $47.00 a Share
(07:25 GMT) *Sumitomo Forestry: $4.28B Acquisition Costs Include Advisory Fees, Other Expenses
(07:42 GMT) *Sumitomo Forestry: Acquisition Price Is $4.1 Billion on Equity Value Basis
(07:46 GMT) Sumitomo Forestry to Buy Tri Pointe Homes for $4.1 Billion

By Kosaku Narioka

Sumitomo Forestry has agreed to acquire U.S. homebuilder Tri Pointe Homes for $4.1 billion.

The Japanese company said Tri Pointe Homes stockholders would receive $47.00 a share.

Sumitomo Forestry said the acquisition would diversify its geographic presence and improve its capacity to deliver homes to U.S. buyers.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Kloeckner & Co Recommends Shareholders Accept Worthington Steel's $2.4 Billion Deal
data.description:
(07:16 GMT) *Kloeckner & Co: Offer Subject to Antitrust Clearance
(07:18 GMT) *Kloeckner Accepts Bid From Worthington Steel
(07:46 GMT) Kloeckner & Co Recommends Shareholders Accept Worthington Steel's $2.4 Billion Deal

By Adam Whittaker

European steel and metal processor Kloeckner & Co recommended shareholders accept a takeover offer from U.S.'s Worthington Steel implying value of $2.4 billion including debt.

The company said it management board and supervisory board both concluded the offer is in the best interest of the company and shareholders.

Worthington is offering shareholders 11 euros ($13.06)in cash per share. This is a roughly 81% premium to the closing price on the Dec. 5, which was the last day before negotiations on a takeover offer were publicly announced.

The offer price implies an enterprise value of $2.4 billion, Worthington said in the middle of January.

Kloeckner's largest shareholder Swoctem has already tendered its entire stake of around 41.53% to Worthington, Kloeckner said.

The deal requires approval from shareholders representing 65% of the company's outstanding shares. The acceptance period ends March 12.

Kloeckner said Worthington doesn't intend to cut jobs upon taking ownership of the company.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Jenoptik falls after weaker preliminary FY results
data.description:
** Shares in Jenoptik fall 11.8% in early Frankfurt trade after the German optical electronic group posted weaker preliminary FY results

** It reports FY sales of 1.05 billion euros ($1.24 billion) with EBITDA at 192 million euros and a margin at 18.4%

** Jefferies says Q4 results are weaker, pointing to consensus miss on both sales and EBITDA by 3%

** "The primary reason for the negative sentiment appears to be the underperformance in the 2025 margin guidance, which is significantly below market expectations and understandably disappointing," Metzler analyst Pal Skirta says

** While Jenoptik didn't publish a quantitative outlook for FY 2026, it says revenue EBITDA margin should increase

** "The improvement in visibility this year could already support sentiment, but ultimately, it will depend on the guidance provided on March 25," the analyst adds

($1 = 0.8438 euros)

data.title: MS&AD Insurance 9-month Net Income Rises
data.description:
BRUSSELS/FRANKFURT/PARIS (dpa-AFX) - MS&AD Insurance Group Holdings (MSADY) reported nine-month net income attributable to owners of the parent of 657.1 billion yen, an increase of 5.0% from last year. Earnings per share was 438.92 yen compared to 400.10 yen. For the nine months ended December 31, 2025, ordinary income was 5.9 trillion yen, an increase of 12.9%.

For the year ending March 31, 2026, the company projects: net income attributable to owners of the parent of 590.0 billion yen; net income attributable to owners of the parent per share of 396.17 yen; and ordinary profit of 834.0 billion yen.

MS&AD Insurance is trading at 4,190 yen, down 0.40%.

data.title: Turkey detains 16 in OnlyFans probe, seizes $6.9 mln in assets
data.description:
Turkey detained 16 people and seized assets worth about 300 million lira ($6.9 million) on Friday in an investigation into alleged money laundering linked to content shared on the OnlyFans platform, prosecutors said.

The probe, led by a department investigating terrorism financing and money laundering crimes, targeted 25 suspects and two companies in operations across eight provinces, including Istanbul, Ankara and Antalya, the Istanbul chief public prosecutor’s office said.

Prosecutors said suspects generated income by sharing explicit content on social media and directing users to paid platforms, including OnlyFans and private messaging channels such as Telegram.

OnlyFans has been blocked in Turkey since June 7, 2023, by a ruling of an Istanbul court on the grounds that it hosted content deemed contrary to public morality and family values.

Despite the ban, suspects were found to have accessed the platform via virtual private networks (VPNs), prosecutors said.

According to the statement, the suspects laundered proceeds through the purchase of assets, as well as investments in bitcoin and gold.

Authorities identified 10 properties, 14 vehicles and two companies linked to the suspects. The total value of the assets was estimated at around 300 million lira.

The prosecutor's office said the investigation was ongoing.

data.title: CCCX Stock Rises After Shareholders Approve Infleqtion Merger With Limited Redemptions
data.description:
Key points:
  • Churchill Capital Corp X shares rose more than 4% in overnight trading after shareholders approved all proposals related to its planned merger with Infleqtion.
  • Shareholders backed the Infleqtion deal with more than 22.1 million votes in favor and limited redemptions of just 0.09% of Class A shares.
  • Ahead of the vote, Barclays has cut its stake below the 5% reporting threshold.

Shares of Churchill Capital Corp X (CCCX) rose more than 4% in overnight trading on Thursday after the SPAC said shareholders approved all proposals related to its planned merger with quantum technology firm Infleqtion.

The deal stood out in the SPAC market because of its low redemption rate. The merger vote preserves nearly $551 million in expected gross proceeds and clears the path for the combined entity to list on the New York Stock Exchange, pending deal closing. 

CCCX stock fell nearly 8% during Thursday’s session before rebounding almost 2% in after-hours trading.

Shareholders Greenlight Infleqtion Merger

In a new filing, Churchill Capital Corp X said it held an extraordinary general meeting on Feb. 12, where shareholders voted in favor of the proposed merger with Infleqtion, along with related proposals covering domestication, governance changes, stock issuance, incentive plans, and director elections.

Roughly 46.6% of the company’s voting power was represented at the meeting, with the merger proposal receiving more than 22.1 million votes in favor, surpassing the votes against and abstentions.

The filing showed that redemptions were limited, with only about 37,800 Class A shares redeemed, representing 0.09% of shares outstanding. As a result, just under $400,000 was removed from the trust account. Churchill expects to deliver about $551.4 million in gross proceeds to Infleqtion at closing, including about $424.8 million from the trust account and $126.5 million from a previously announced private placement.

Following approval of the domestication proposal, Churchill said it will change its jurisdiction of incorporation from the Cayman Islands to Delaware. Upon completion of the transaction, the combined company will be renamed Infleqtion, Inc. Churchill plans to apply for the post-merger company’s common stock to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol INFQ.

Barclays Cut Stake Ahead Of The Merger Vote 

In a Wednesday filing, Barclays reduced its stake below the 5% reporting threshold ahead of the vote. The filing marked a reduction from Barclays’ prior disclosure in November, when the bank reported ownership of 3 million shares, or 8.63% of the company. The latest filing showed that Barclays trimmed its position by roughly 1.2 million shares, bringing its stake below the 5% reporting threshold.

Fueling The Quantum Momentum

Earlier in the week, CCCX surged more than 15% to mark its best day in about three months following Infleqtion’s announcement of a partnership with NASA to help develop the "world’s first quantum gravity sensor" into space.

Infleqtion has positioned itself as a diversified quantum technology company, with products spanning quantum sensors, clocks, RF systems, and inertial-sensing equipment. Government and state-funded entities account for more than half of its revenue, with contracts linked to areas such as energy grids and locks.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment for CCCX was ‘extremely bullish’ amid ‘extremely high’ message volume.

One user said, “INFQ- Get those space suits on we going to SPACE. Literally speaking. The future looks bright. We should pass RGTI and IONQ on the way up!”

https://stocktwits.com/MRBills824/message/644885845

Another user said, “love that we'll have a established defense contractor supply our products for us. Infleqtion will be in subs, ships, planes, drones, etc.”

https://stocktwits.com/spacpack89/message/644884144

CCCX stock has declined 29% over the past 12 months.

data.title: CBOT corn may fall into $4.26-3/4 to $4.27-3/4 range
data.description:
CBOT March corn (CH26) may fall into $4.26-3/4 to $4.27-3/4 range, as it failed to break resistance at $4.31-3/4.

The failure suggests a completion of the bounce from $4.25. Due to this failure, the downtrend from $4.36 may resume. The target zone of $4.26-3/4 to $4.27-3/4 will be confirmed when the market breaks $4.29-1/4.

A break above $4.30-1/2 could lead to a gain into $4.31-3/4 to $4.33-1/2 range. On the daily chart, the rise from $4.25-1/4 will be considered a pullback toward a rising trendline, unless the contract breaks above $4.32-3/4.

The pattern from $4.48-1/2 still looks like a bearish flag, despite the weak bounce from $4.25-1/4. A close below the Thursday low of $4.27 would confirm the pattern.

** Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. The views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses. ​

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 1:10 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 1% to 82,841, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 fell 1% to 25,540, with information technology stocks dropping as much as 5%, mirroring losses on Wall Street and Asian markets amid a global tech selloff.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee declined 0.16% versus the U.S. dollar to 90.7325, weighed down by dollar demand spurred by maturing positions in the non-deliverable forwards market and a risk-off mood, as jitters about the information technology sector led to a slump in global equities.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 98.625 rupees, with the yield down 1 bp at 6.6742% as profit taking offset positivity form federal government's debt switch with RBI, which will reduce gross borrowing for the next fiscal year.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was a 1.5 bps higher at 5.5125%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) eased 1.25 bps to 6.075%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 4.80%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.00%

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on February 13
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening higher on Friday, with futures up 0.3%.

* NATWEST: NatWest said its annual profit rose 24% on Friday, slightly ahead of forecasts, and the lender set out more ambitious performance targets as it expands in Britain's costly but potentially lucrative wealth management market.

* WORKER'S PAY: More than 1.4 million National Health Service workers in Britain will receive a 3.3% pay rise from April, the government said.

* ITV: Comcast-owned Sky's talks with ITV about buying its broadcast channels and streaming platform have slowed in recent weeks, three people with knowledge of the matter said, as the battle to buy Warner Bros Discovery disrupts the industry.

* OIL: Oil prices were little changed after falling in the previous session, and are set for their second weekly decline, on receding concerns of an Iranian conflict that could affect supply and on forecasts supply will exceed demand this year.

* METALS: Shanghai copper declined amid a broader sell-off, as Chinese investors left on the last trading day before the long Lunar New Year break.

* GOLD: Gold rebounded, recovering from a nearly one-week low in the previous session, as investors awaited key U.S. inflation figures for guidance on interest rate direction following robust jobs data that reduced expectations of rate cuts

data.title: Asahi Intecc Co., Ltd. H1 Profit Climbs
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - Asahi Intecc Co., Ltd. (6XT.F) released earnings for its first half that Increased, from the same period last year

The company's earnings came in at JPY17.214 billion, or JPY64.56 per share. This compares with JPY12.261 billion, or JPY45.14 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 15.9% to JPY71.266 billion from JPY61.491 billion last year.

Asahi Intecc Co., Ltd. earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: JPY17.214 Bln. vs. JPY12.261 Bln. last year.

-EPS: JPY64.56 vs. JPY45.14 last year.

-Revenue: JPY71.266 Bln vs. JPY61.491 Bln last year.

-Guidance:

Full year EPS guidance: JPY 114.88

Full year revenue guidance: JPY 141.142 B

data.title: Trump Plans to Scale Back Some Steel, Aluminum Tariffs, FT Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• President Trump is planning to roll back some tariffs on steel and aluminum products, the Financial Times reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The Trump administration plans to exempt some items affected by the tariffs and launch more targeted probes into specific products, according to the FT.
  • The White House, the Commerce Department and the U.S. Trade Representative's office declined to comment, according to the FT.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/2wup7u9z

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com
